channel.title: Health - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: People who stay mentally sharp well into their 80s don't do crossword puzzles or brain games — they all quit doing these 6 things that most people never realize are slowly eroding their cognitive flexibility - Silicon Canals
item.description:
1. People who stay mentally sharp well into their 80s don't do crossword puzzles or brain games — they all quit doing these 6 things that most people never realize are slowly eroding their cognitive flexibility  Silicon Canals
  2. Psychology says people who stay mentally sharp past 70 don't do it through brain games — they do it through a single daily practice that most people abandon the moment they retire  VegOut
  3. Most people who stay intellectually alive past 70 don't follow the advice they're given about brain health — when psychologists studied them, these 7 counterintuitive habits kept showing up, and not one of them involved puzzles or memory games  VegOut
  4. Psychology says people who stay mentally sharp past 70 almost always maintained one specific habit that has nothing to do with education — they kept having conversations with people who disagreed with them, and that friction kept their reasoning muscles  VegOut
